subtopics.subtopic_description: Educational background or training
notes: Attended college for several semesters but did not complete a degree
Took courses in accounting, business administration, and graphic design
Participated in various online workshops and webinars on e-commerce and digital marketing
Self-taught in design software through online tutorials and manuals

subtopics.subtopic_description: Specific job title and role description
notes: Operates as an Online Merchant and Owner
Designs personalized books and stationery for events
Manages both a marketplace storefront and a standalone e-commerce website
Oversees end-to-end customer order processing, vendor coordination, and business bookkeeping

subtopics.subtopic_description: Current industry or sector (e.g., tech, finance, manufacturing)
notes: Works in the online retail and e-commerce sector
Specializes in the personalized gifts and stationery niche
Operates within the broader creative and design services industry

subtopics.subtopic_description: Company size and environment
notes: Runs a sole proprietorship with no full-time employees
Works closely with a small network of specialized print vendors
Occasionally collaborates with freelance designers or assistants for large projects

subtopics.subtopic_description: Type of business or market segment
notes: Focuses on B2C (business-to-consumer) sales
Targets customers planning events such as weddings, birthdays, and corporate gatherings
Offers highly customized, made-to-order products
Operates primarily through online channels (Etsy, own website)

subtopics.subtopic_description: Duration/years of experience in current role
notes: Has over 10 years of experience as an online merchant
Started the e-commerce business after previous experience in accounting
Has witnessed and adapted to multiple changes in the online retail landscape over the past decade

subtopics.subtopic_description: Professional seniority or career level
notes: Acts as business owner and senior decision-maker
Performs all core business functions without direct supervision
Regarded as an experienced professional and subject matter expert in personalized stationery e-commerce

subtopics.subtopic_description: Primary job responsibilities and regular daily tasks
notes: Monitors online stores for incoming customer orders and inquiries
Designs personalized product layouts using Adobe and Serif software
Coordinates order fulfillment with print vendors through proprietary ordering systems
Communicates with customers to confirm order details and address any issues
Maintains daily bookkeeping and profitability tracking in Google Sheets

subtopics.subtopic_description: Approximate proportion of time spent on core activities
notes: Spends approximately 40% of time on bookkeeping and record-keeping tasks
Devotes around 30% of time to design work for new and existing orders
Allocates about 20% of time to order processing and vendor coordination
Spends the remaining 10% on customer service, marketing, and website management

subtopics.subtopic_description: Level of autonomy and scope of decision-making in the role
notes: Makes all business decisions independently, including product design, pricing, and vendor selection
Handles customer issues and refunds without escalation to others
Determines marketing strategies, promotions, and product launches
Responsible for all compliance and financial reporting for the business

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks that feel easiest or most natural to perform
notes: Daily bookkeeping and accounting tasks come naturally due to prior training
Communicating with customers and confirming order details is straightforward
Navigating the Etsy and Equid dashboards is intuitive after years of use

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks perceived as most challenging or complex
notes: Designing unique layouts for highly personalized orders can be creatively demanding
Managing multiple custom orders with different deadlines requires careful organization
Occasional technical issues with vendor ordering systems can be time-consuming to resolve

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks that are repetitive, data-heavy, or suitable for automation
notes: Daily entry of sales and expenses into spreadsheets is highly repetitive
Monitoring order statuses and shipment tracking could be automated
Reconciling vendor invoices with customer orders involves repetitive data checks

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks that are most enjoyable or engaging versus those that feel boring or tedious
notes: Enjoys creative design work and developing new product ideas
Finds customer interactions rewarding, especially when receiving positive feedback
Considers repetitive bookkeeping and order entry to be tedious but necessary

subtopics.subtopic_description: Common pain points or inefficiencies in completing tasks
notes: Manual bookkeeping consumes significant time each day
Lack of integration between sales platforms and vendor systems leads to double data entry
Tracking order shipment status across multiple vendors is inefficient and error-prone

subtopics.subtopic_description: How enjoyment, skill level, and productivity relate to one another
notes: High proficiency in accounting and bookkeeping increases productivity but does not enhance enjoyment
Creative design tasks are enjoyable but more time-consuming and require deep focus
Productivity is highest when working on familiar, routine tasks, even if they are less engaging

subtopics.subtopic_description: Attitude towards learning new technologies and tools
notes: Open to learning new tools that offer clear business benefits
Prefers technologies that are intuitive and easy to implement
Has a pragmatic approach, weighing learning curve against potential time savings

subtopics.subtopic_description: Perceived adaptability to new software/methods
notes: Adapts to new software at a moderate pace, relying on self-study and online resources
Comfortable experimenting with new design tools and e-commerce features
Occasionally seeks help from online forums or vendor support if stuck

subtopics.subtopic_description: Willingness to invest time in tech training
notes: Willing to invest time in training if a tool will significantly improve workflow
Prefers short, focused tutorials over lengthy courses
Prioritizes learning tools that directly impact daily tasks

subtopics.subtopic_description: Motivations or barriers to learning new tech (e.g., workload, relevance)
notes: Motivated by the potential to save time on repetitive tasks
High daily workload can be a barrier to dedicating time for training
Needs to see clear, relevant applications to the business before adopting new tech

subtopics.subtopic_description: Influence of peers or management on willingness to adopt new tools
notes: As a sole proprietor, decisions to adopt new tools are self-driven
Occasionally learns about new tools from online merchant communities
Vendor-provided updates or recommendations can prompt tool adoption

topic: Primary Tools and Technologies Used in Work

subtopics.subtopic_description: Specific software, platforms, or systems used daily
notes: Uses Etsy Shop Manager dashboard to process orders and manage inventory
Maintains a standalone website on the Equid platform for direct sales
Relies on Google Docs and Google Sheets for record-keeping and accounting
Employs Adobe Photoshop, Affinity Suite, and Serif Page Plus for design work

subtopics.subtopic_description: Essential non-AI tools for workflow
notes: Proprietary vendor ordering software for submitting print jobs
Email and messaging tools for customer communication
Manual spreadsheets for tax and expense tracking

subtopics.subtopic_description: Familiarity with industry-standard technologies
notes: Proficient in mainstream e-commerce platforms such as Etsy and Equid
Experienced with Adobe Creative Suite and Affinity design tools
Prefers manual accounting over industry-standard accounting software (e.g., QuickBooks)

subtopics.subtopic_description: Interoperability or integration issues between tools
notes: No direct integration between sales platforms (Etsy, Equid) and vendor ordering systems
Must manually transfer order details from storefronts to vendor portals
Bookkeeping data from sales platforms is not automatically synced with financial records

subtopics.subtopic_description: Familiarity with fundamental AI/ML concepts and terminology
notes: Understands basic concepts such as machine learning and natural language processing
Has read about generative AI models like ChatGPT in industry news
Recognizes the distinction between AI automation and rule-based scripts

subtopics.subtopic_description: Names of specific AI software/platforms currently used in work
notes: Has occasionally used ChatGPT for drafting customer messages and product descriptions
Tried Grammarly's AI-powered suggestions for proofreading listing content
Experimented with Canva's AI design features for quick mockups

subtopics.subtopic_description: Frequency and purpose of AI tool application (specific use cases)
notes: Uses AI tools sporadically, primarily for content generation and editing tasks
Consults LLMs to brainstorm product ideas or solve minor technical issues
Utilizes AI features for efficiency when updating large batches of product listings

subtopics.subtopic_description: Specific examples of AI success and failure experiences (lessons learned)
notes: Successfully used ChatGPT to draft a challenging customer apology email
Found AI-powered design suggestions in Canva occasionally mismatched to customer style preferences
Learned that AI-generated content often needs significant editing for tone and accuracy

subtopics.subtopic_description: Availability of organizational training or peer resources for AI use
notes: No formal AI training provided (as a sole proprietor)
Relies on online tutorials, forums, and community advice for AI tool learning
Occasionally exchanges AI tool tips with other online merchants via social media groups

topic: AI Interaction Style and Workflow Change

subtopics.subtopic_description: Preferred mode of interaction: independent versus step-by-step collaboration
notes: Prefers to use AI tools independently for specific tasks, then review results
Finds step-by-step guided AI useful when exploring new workflows
Values flexibility to switch between autonomous use and collaborative AI support

subtopics.subtopic_description: Style of human-AI teaming (e.g., advisor, assistant, co-worker)
notes: Views AI primarily as an assistant for drafting and editing
Occasionally uses AI as an advisor for brainstorming solutions to business problems
Does not view AI as a co-worker but as a supplementary tool

subtopics.subtopic_description: Willingness and openness to adopting new AI-driven workflows
notes: Open to adopting AI-driven workflows that offer clear productivity gains
Skeptical of fully automated workflows for creative or customer-facing tasks
Willing to experiment with AI in low-risk areas before fully integrating into core processes

subtopics.subtopic_description: Preference for conversational vs. command-based interfaces (communication dynamics)
notes: Prefers conversational interfaces for brainstorming and content creation
Finds command-based interfaces more efficient for structured tasks (e.g., data extraction)
Appreciates having both options available depending on task complexity

subtopics.subtopic_description: Extent to which tasks rely on specialized, tacit domain knowledge
notes: Product personalization relies heavily on understanding customer intent and event context
Design work depends on years of experience with typography and aesthetics
Bookkeeping tasks require practical knowledge of retail cash flow and tax implications

subtopics.subtopic_description: Level of trust in AI outputs for work tasks and critical decisions
notes: Trusts AI for initial drafts or suggestions, but always reviews output before finalizing
Does not rely on AI for critical financial or customer-facing decisions
Requires manual verification for any automated order or accounting processes

subtopics.subtopic_description: Ideal balance of human effort and AI automation for specific tasks
notes: Prefers AI automation for repetitive or data-heavy tasks (e.g., bookkeeping, order status checks)
Retains human control over creative design and customer communication
Seeks a workflow where AI handles routine monitoring, but exceptions are escalated for manual review

subtopics.subtopic_description: Conditions under which high automation is acceptable or threatening
notes: Accepts high automation for back-office operations if override options are available
Finds full automation of customer interaction or design potentially risky to business reputation
Welcomes automation as long as it is transparent and can be manually audited

topic: AI Impact on Skills and Job Security

subtopics.subtopic_description: Perceived impact of AI on the importance of existing skills (enhanced vs. reduced)
notes: Sees AI as enhancing efficiency in administrative and accounting skills
Believes creative design and customer empathy remain core, irreplaceable skills
Anticipates some reduction in time spent on routine tasks, allowing more focus on high-value work

subtopics.subtopic_description: Emerging skills or new areas of responsibility created by AI
notes: Learning to oversee and audit automated workflows is an emerging responsibility
Developing prompt engineering and AI tool evaluation skills
Staying current with new AI features and their business applications

subtopics.subtopic_description: Level of concern about AI replacing specific tasks or the overall role
notes: Mildly concerned about automation of basic bookkeeping but sees core role as safe
Confident that creative and customer-focused aspects of the business are less likely to be replaced
Views AI as a tool to augment, not eliminate, personal expertise

subtopics.subtopic_description: Availability of override mechanisms or manual checks for AI-driven processes
notes: Always reviews AI-generated outputs before customer delivery
Keeps manual logs and backup records for all critical business data
Prefers AI solutions that include clear audit trails and rollback options

subtopics.subtopic_description: Perceived change in team or company policies regarding AI adoption
notes: As sole proprietor, decisions on AI adoption are self-determined
Has become more open to exploring AI integrations over the past few years
Follows industry trends and best practices from online merchant communities

subtopics.subtopic_description: General outlook on AI's broader societal and industry impact
notes: Believes AI will drive efficiency and lower barriers to entry for small business owners
Acknowledges risks of job displacement in roles with high automation potential
Expects AI to foster new creative opportunities in product design and marketing

subtopics.subtopic_description: Personal beliefs about the ethics and risks of AI in the workplace
notes: Concerned about data privacy and the use of customer information by AI tools
Believes AI should augment, not replace, human judgment in creative work
Worries about potential bias or errors in automated decision-making

subtopics.subtopic_description: Missing AI tools or features that would be most beneficial in the future
notes: Desires an AI bookkeeping assistant that automatically reconciles orders, expenses, and tax records
Wants a tool to monitor vendor order fulfillment and alert to potential shipping delays
Would benefit from a seamless integration between e-commerce platforms and vendor systems

subtopics.subtopic_description: Predicted evolution of their job in the next 5-10 years with AI integration
notes: Anticipates spending less time on manual record-keeping and more on product innovation
Expects AI to handle routine monitoring, freeing up time for business growth activities
Foresees increased need to manage and audit automated workflows

subtopics.subtopic_description: Concrete steps they would want their organization to take regarding AI strategy
notes: Would prioritize investment in AI tools that integrate with current sales and vendor platforms
Seeks ongoing education and resources to stay updated on AI best practices
Prefers to implement AI gradually, starting with low-risk back-office functions
